- Add updater:default capability permission
- Windows passive install mode

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(blazor): fix WebSocket corruption from response compression + CSS error

Root cause: UseResponseCompression() wrapped the response stream BEFORE
MapRazorComponents/MapHub, corrupting WebSocket frames. Circuit connected
but immediately crashed: Server returned an error on close.

Fix: UseWhen(!IsWebSocketRequest) skips compression for WebSocket upgrades.

Also: removed nonexistent GoldAIG.Web.styles.css ref (MIME error), fixed
deprecated meta tag, enabled SignalR EnableDetailedErrors in non-prod.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Test262 25% pass rate milestone (closes #254) (#379)

Wire builtins to the test262 runner's global environment by calling
install_globals(), include assert.js by default per the Test262 spec,
add function property side-table so that ad-hoc properties on Function
values (e.g. assert.sameValue) work correctly, support PlainObject
constructors in the Construct opcode, expand the unsupported-features
skip list for features the engine does not yet implement, run each test
on a dedicated thread to isolate stack overflows, lower
MAX_CALL_STACK_DEPTH to 512 to prevent native stack exhaustion, and
add clear_call_stack() for test harness cleanup.

Raise the CI test262 threshold from 0.0% to 25.0%.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Misaligned relocation tables

Loader test coverage: 58.2% → 58.7%

commit.message:
Fix: mark pre-existing jobs as sheets_row_pushed to prevent duplicates

The sheets_row_pushed migration added the column with DEFAULT 0, causing
all pre-existing local DB jobs to be re-appended to the sheet as new rows.

- New column migration now backfills existing jobs as pushed (=1)
- One-time fix-up for deployed DBs where column already exists:
  sets sheets_row_pushed=1 for all jobs created before 2026-03-06

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update 1.Enrichment/1.TRANSACTION.es.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add missing environment variables to hosted agent container

The container was crashing immediately on startup with:
  System.InvalidOperationException: AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID environment variable is required

The azd agent extension auto-injects AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT, AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT_NAME,
and STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME, but the app also requires AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID,
RULES_STORAGE_URL, and AZURE_AI_PROJECT_ENDPOINT which were not being passed through.

Add environment_variables section to azure.yaml container config using envsubst
syntax to resolve values from azd environment outputs at deploy time.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Show welcome message at least five times before suppressing (#48)

* Initial plan

* Show welcome message at least five times instead of once

Replace the single marker file approach with a counter-based approach
in both 00-remix.sh and 00-remix.fish. The marker file now stores a
count that is incremented each time the message is shown. The message
is suppressed only after it has been displayed 5 times.

Co-authored-by: crramirez <8397379+crramirez@users.noreply.github.com>

* Improve robustness of fish shell marker file reading with string trim

Co-authored-by: crramirez <8397379+crramirez@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: validate numeric input, clean up vars on early return, remove dead code

- 00-remix.sh: Replace arithmetic coercion with POSIX case statement
  for safe numeric validation of marker file content
- 00-remix.sh: Add unset calls before early return to prevent variable
  leakage into user's shell environment
- 00-remix.fish: Remove dead '; or echo 0' from cat|string trim pipeline

commit.message:
feat: PreApprovedTools visibility fix, self-write suppression, schedule persistence

- Fix PreApprovedTools not visible to models: tool filter now includes
  pre-approved tools so the OnPreToolUse hook can auto-approve them
- Wire PreApprovedTools through AgentSession, ScheduledTask, and scheduler
- Add content-hash self-write suppression to DebouncedFileWatcher so
  programmatic config saves don't trigger reload loops
- Wire NotifySelfWrite in AgentFactory.SaveConfig/SavePromptText and
  UserPreferences.Save
- Fix ScheduleStore.Save to use WriteOptions (pretty-printed JSON)

commit.message:
fix(actions): make deletePortfolio atomic by catching P2003 FK error

The previous count-then-delete pattern had a TOCTOU race: a transaction
could be inserted after the count and before the delete, causing the DB
to throw a generic error instead of the user-facing message.

Fix: attempt the delete directly and map Prisma P2003 (foreign key
constraint violation) to the friendly 'has transactions' message.
All other errors still surface as generic failures.

Updated tests to mock the FK error path rather than the count mock.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use unique coverage filenames per test project (#32)

## Problem

All test projects were writing their coverage output to the same file
(`coverage.cobertura.xml`), causing each project to overwrite the
previous one's report.

## Solution

- Removed `--coverage-output coverage.cobertura.xml` from the `dotnet
test` command so that the test runner auto-generates a unique
(GUID-based) filename per project.
- Updated the ReportGenerator reports glob from
`**/coverage/**/coverage.cobertura.xml` to
`**/coverage/**/*.cobertura.xml` to pick up all uniquely-named coverage
files.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add TLS module ID limit to prevent unbounded growth

Implements IMPROVEMENT_FINDINGS.md section 6.2 (No Limits on Module ID Growth).

Changes:
- Add maxModuleID constant (65536) to cap the number of TLS modules
- Check module ID limit before incrementing in RegisterModule()
- Returns clear error if limit exceeded

This prevents:
- Unbounded memory growth in the DTV (Dynamic Thread Vector)
- Module ID overflow on 32-bit systems
- Memory waste with huge module IDs used as array indices

Impact: Minimal complexity increase (5.7 overall, well below threshold of 10)
Tests: All tests pass (go test ./...)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(api): standardise Paddle webhook responses with formatSuccess/formatError (M-02)

Replaced 6 raw NextResponse.json() calls with formatSuccess() and
formatError() from the shared API response formatter. All webhook
responses now follow the standard { success, data/error, timestamp }
shape, consistent with every other API route.

Error responses now use ErrorHandlingService.createError() return
values directly (StructuredError) instead of discarding them and
returning ad-hoc { error: '...' } objects.

Paddle only checks HTTP status codes, so this is backward-compatible.

commit.message:
fix(forms): replace form action with onSubmit to prevent auto-reset on error

React 19 auto-resets uncontrolled form fields after a form action
completes. Using 'action={clientFn}' with defaultValue inputs means
the user's input is wiped on a failed submission. Switch to onSubmit
+ e.preventDefault() which does not trigger the auto-reset behaviour.

Affected: AssetForm, PortfolioForm

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

All 6 cross_module analysis files used 'magpie-agent/' as a path prefix
in their Related Documentation sections (e.g., 'magpie-agent/modules/
module_52.md'). Since the agent's working directory IS magpie-agent/,

which doesn't exist.

- carbon_balance_conservation.md (4)
- circular_dependency_resolution.md (7)
- land_balance_conservation.md (9)
- modification_safety_guide.md (11)
- nitrogen_food_balance.md (8)

commit.message:
feat: add read-only beneficiario detail page /beneficiarios/[id]

- New dedicated detail page showing personal data, demographics,
  geographic location, and attendance history
- Beneficiary name in list table is now a clickable link to detail
- Added eye icon button in actions column for quick access
- Edit button on detail page navigates to /beneficiarios?edit=<id>
- Wrapped BeneficiariosPage in Suspense for useSearchParams support

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: stale directory trees, ghost paths, unsafe cd patterns

README.md structural fixes:
- Directory tree was missing agent/helpers/ (9 helpers, major system component)
- core_docs listed 4 files but has 6 (missing Query_Patterns, Response_Guidelines)
- reference/ listed 6 files but has 9 (missing 3 non-GAMS guides)
- Tree showed non-existent archive/ directory, now shows scripts/
- Fixed stale path core_docs/phase2_dependency_analysis → reference/
- Removed stale session archive workflow (archive/ dir never existed)
- Updated core_docs label from 'Phase 0: Foundation' to actual description

AGENT.md:
- Directory layout was missing agent/helpers/ directory
- Fixed cd-based git command to use git -C pattern (Step 1b)

sync.md:
- Converted 3 bash blocks from cd-based to git -C pattern
  (same class of bug as session_startup.md Step 0)

Pattern learned: directory trees and file counts in documentation
are highly prone to drift. The validator checks file counts for
modules/cross_module/GAMS refs but not for core_docs or reference.

All 26 validation checks pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
